meta.title: Advanced Grammar: Tense, Aspect & Modality
meta.description: Master complex tense, aspect, and modal structures for precise communication.
meta.description_ko: 정확한 의사소통을 위한 복잡한 시제, 상, 양상 구조를 학습합니다.

explanation.en: Aspect = whether an action is complete (perfect), ongoing (continuous), or habitual.
explanation.ko: aspect = 행동이 완료됐는지(완료상), 진행 중인지(진행상), 습관적인지를 나타냄.

questions.prompt: 'aspect'는 문법에서 무엇을 나타냅니까?
choices: 행동이 완료, 진행, 반복 중 어떤 상태인지를 나타냄

explanation.en: Past perfect continuous: action ongoing up to a specific past moment.
explanation.ko: 과거 완료 진행형: 특정 과거 시점까지 진행 중이던 행동.

questions.prompt: By the time she arrived, the meeting _____ for an hour.

explanation.en: 'Will have been + verb-ing' = future perfect continuous — ongoing action by a future point.
explanation.ko: 'will have been + 동사-ing' = 미래 완료 진행형 — 미래 시점까지 진행 중인 행동.

questions.prompt: 'will have been working' — 어떤 시제/상인가요?

explanation.en: Future perfect continuous: duration of an action up to a future point.
explanation.ko: 미래 완료 진행형: 미래 시점까지의 행동 기간.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: By next year, she will have been teaching for three decades.

explanation.en: 'Used to' = past states AND habits. 'Would' = repeated past actions only (NOT states).
explanation.ko: 'used to' = 과거 상태와 습관. 'would' = 반복적 과거 행동만(상태 동사에 불가).

questions.prompt: 'I used to smoke' vs 'I would smoke every evening' — 차이는?
choices: 'used to'는 과거 상태나 습관, 'would'는 과거 반복적 행동(상태 동사에는 사용 불가)

explanation.en: 'Must have + past participle' = logical deduction about the past.
explanation.ko: 'must have + 과거분사' = 과거에 대한 논리적 추론.

questions.prompt: She must have _____ the wrong train — she's two hours late.

explanation.en: 'Might have' = past possibility. 'Could have' = past ability/possibility or unfulfilled potential.
explanation.ko: 'might have' = 과거 가능성. 'could have' = 과거 능력/가능성 또는 실현되지 않은 잠재력.

questions.prompt: 'might have done' vs 'could have done' — 주요 차이는?
choices: 'might have' = 과거 가능성(50% 이하), 'could have' = 과거 능력 또는 가능성
'might have' = 과거 후회, 'could have' = 과거 의무

explanation.en: Key tense structures and their temporal meanings.
explanation.ko: 주요 시제 구조와 시간적 의미.

questions.prompt: 시제 구조와 의미를 연결하세요.

explanation.en: Future perfect passive: action completed before a future deadline.
explanation.ko: 미래 완료 수동태: 미래 마감 전에 완료되는 행동.

questions.prompt: The report _____ by the committee before the deadline tomorrow.

explanation.en: 'Hardly + had + subject + past participle + when/before + past simple'.
explanation.ko: 'Hardly + had + 주어 + 과거분사 + when/before + 단순 과거'.

questions.prompt: 'hardly' 뒤에 오는 구조는?
choices: 과거 완료 도치 + when/before + 단순 과거

explanation.en: Future perfect: action completed before a future reference point.
explanation.ko: 미래 완료: 미래 참조 시점 전에 완료되는 행동.

questions.prompt: By the time you read this, I _____ for the airport.

explanation.en: 'Needn't have + past participle' = did something unnecessary (it was done but wasn't needed).
explanation.ko: 'needn't have + 과거분사' = 불필요한 일을 했다(했지만 필요 없었던).

questions.prompt: 'I needn't have cooked so much food.' — 이 문장의 의미는?
choices: 음식을 많이 요리할 필요가 없었는데 실제로 요리했다
음식을 요리하지 않아도 됐고 실제로 하지 않았다

explanation.en: 'Ought to have + past participle' = should have done (mild criticism of past action).
explanation.ko: 'ought to have + 과거분사' = 했어야 했다(과거 행동에 대한 가벼운 비판).

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: She ought to have informed the committee before making the decision.

explanation.en: 'Was supposed to' = was expected to happen but didn't.
explanation.ko: 'was supposed to' = 일어날 것으로 예상됐지만 실제로는 일어나지 않았다.

questions.prompt: The situation was _____ to improve, but instead it deteriorated further.

explanation.en: 'Belong' is stative — can't say 'I am belonging to'. Stative verbs describe states, not actions.
explanation.ko: 'belong'은 상태 동사 — 'I am belonging to'라고 할 수 없다.

questions.prompt: 다음 중 상태 동사(stative verb)로 진행형을 쓸 수 없는 동사는?

explanation.en: Past perfect continuous: ongoing action up to a specific past moment.
explanation.ko: 과거 완료 진행형: 특정 과거 시점까지 진행 중이던 행동.

questions.prompt: He had been _____ in the rain for over an hour before help arrived.

explanation.en: 'Should have known better' = criticism of oneself for not behaving more wisely.
explanation.ko: 'should have known better' = 더 현명하게 행동했어야 했는데 그러지 못했다는 자기 비판.

questions.prompt: 'I should have known better.' — 이 문장의 의미는?

explanation.en: 'For + duration' not 'since + duration'. 'Since' needs a specific starting point.
explanation.ko: 'for + 기간'이지 'since + 기간'이 아닙니다. 'since'는 특정 시작 시점이 필요합니다.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'She has been living in Seoul since five years.'

explanation.en: Future perfect: completed action by a specified future time.
explanation.ko: 미래 완료: 지정된 미래 시간까지 완료되는 행동.

questions.prompt: The company _____ three new offices by the end of this year.

explanation.en: 'Would have + pp' = result clause in 3rd conditional OR speculation about the past.
explanation.ko: 'would have + 과거분사' = 3형 조건문 결과절 또는 과거에 대한 추측.

questions.prompt: 'would have + past participle'의 주요 용도는?
choices: 3형 조건문의 결과절 또는 과거에 대한 후회

explanation.en: 'Considering + clause' = participle clause giving context/reason.
explanation.ko: 'considering + 절' = 맥락/이유를 제공하는 분사 구문.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Considering how hard she had worked, the result was disappointing.

explanation.en: Past perfect continuous: ongoing action before a past event.
explanation.ko: 과거 완료 진행형: 과거 사건 이전에 진행 중이던 행동.

questions.prompt: It _____ for over three hours when the power finally came back on.

explanation.en: After 'want/need/would like + object', use TO-infinitive: 'I want you TO go'.
explanation.ko: 'want/need/would like + 목적어' 다음에는 TO 부정사: 'I want you TO go'.

questions.prompt: 'bare infinitive'를 사용하는 경우가 아닌 것은?

explanation.en: Modal perfect structures for reasoning about past events.
explanation.ko: 과거 사건에 대한 추론을 위한 양상 완료 구조.

questions.prompt: 양상 동사와 과거 추론 표현을 연결하세요.

explanation.en: 'Didn't know' = simple past for an explanation of why something didn't happen.
explanation.ko: 'didn't know' = 무언가가 일어나지 않은 이유를 설명하는 단순 과거.

questions.prompt: He _____ it was a public holiday — otherwise he would have come to work.
